turns.text: Q3 fiscal 2026 delivered cloud revenue of $10.2 billion, up 31% in constant currency — our first double-digit quarterly cloud revenue. OCI grew 62%. RPO stands at $170 billion. We now expect full fiscal year 2026 cloud revenue to reach $52 billion, above our raised guidance of $50 billion. The Microsoft expanded partnership announced in January is already generating new OCI workloads from seven Fortune 500 companies in regulated industries.

Fiscal year 2029 cloud revenue of $48 billion is no longer our target — it is our current trajectory. We now expect fiscal 2027 cloud revenue to reach $65 billion and fiscal 2029 cloud revenue to exceed $100 billion. No other enterprise cloud vendor is growing this fast at this scale. Our technology advantages in AI infrastructure, database, and agent software are compounding, not eroding.
Our next question comes from Brent Thill at Jefferies.

Larry, $100 billion in fiscal 2029 cloud revenue from a $25 billion base in 2025 implies roughly 40% compounded growth for four years. What is the primary constraint that could prevent that — capacity, sales motion, or competition?

statements.claim: Oracle Q3 fiscal 2026 cloud revenue was $10.2 billion, the first double-digit quarterly cloud revenue in company history.
Oracle now expects full fiscal year 2026 cloud revenue to reach $52 billion, above the $50 billion raised guidance.
Oracle expects fiscal 2027 cloud revenue to reach $65 billion and fiscal 2029 cloud revenue to exceed $100 billion.
The expanded Microsoft partnership is already generating new OCI workloads from seven Fortune 500 companies in regulated industries.
OCI revenue grew 62% year-over-year in Q3 fiscal 2026.
